questions.questionText: Let y = y(x) be a solution curve of the differential equation $$(y + 1){\tan ^2}x\,dx + \tan x\,dy + y\,dx = 0$$, $$x \in \left( {0,{\pi  \over 2}} \right)$$. If $$\mathop {\lim }\limits_{x \to 0 + } xy(x) = 1$$, then the value of $$y\left( {{\pi  \over 4}} \right)$$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let the solution curve of the differential equation $$x \mathrm{~d} y=\left(\sqrt{x^{2}+y^{2}}+y\right) \mathrm{d} x, x>0$$, intersect the line $$x=1$$ at $$y=0$$ and the line $$x=2$$ at $$y=\alpha$$. Then the value of $$\alpha$$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
If $$y=y(x), x \in(0, \pi / 2)$$ be the solution curve of the differential equation

$$\left(\sin ^{2} 2 x\right) \frac{d y}{d x}+\left(8 \sin ^{2} 2 x+2 \sin 4 x\right) y=2 \mathrm{e}^{-4 x}(2 \sin 2 x+\cos 2 x)$$,

with $$y(\pi / 4)=\mathrm{e}^{-\pi}$$, then $$y(\pi / 6)$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$y=y_{1}(x)$$ and $$y=y_{2}(x)$$ be the solution curves of the differential equation $$\frac{d y}{d x}=y+7$$ with initial conditions $$y_{1}(0)=0$$ and $$y_{2}(0)=1$$ respectively. Then the curves $$y=y_{1}(x)$$ and $$y=y_{2}(x)$$ intersect at
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$y = y(x)$$ be the solution curve of the differential equation $${{dy} \over {dx}} = {y \over x}\left( {1 + x{y^2}(1 + {{\log }_e}x)} \right),x > 0,y(1) = 3$$. Then $${{{y^2}(x)} \over 9}$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let $y=y(x)$ be the solution of the differential equation

$\frac{\mathrm{d} y}{\mathrm{~d} x}=2 x(x+y)^3-x(x+y)-1, y(0)=1$.

Then, $\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}+y\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)\right)^2$ equals :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If $x=x(t)$ is the solution of the differential equation $(t+1) \mathrm{d} x=\left(2 x+(t+1)^4\right) \mathrm{dt}, x(0)=2$, then, $x(1)$ equals _________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$y=y(x)$$ be the solution of the differential equation $$(x^2-3y^2)dx+3xy~dy=0,y(1)=1$$. Then $$6y^2(e)$$ is equal to
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let $y=y(x)$ be the solution curve of the differential equation

$x\left(x^2+e^x\right) d y+\left(\mathrm{e}^x(x-2) y-x^3\right) \mathrm{d} x=0, x>0$, passing through the point $(1,0)$. Then $y(2)$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let the solution curve of the differential equation $x d y-y d x=\sqrt{x^2+y^2} d x, x>0$, $y(1)=0$, be $y=y(x)$. Then $y(3)$ is equal to
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
A curve passes through the point $$\left( {1,{\pi \over 6}} \right)$$. Let the slope of
the curve at each point $$(x,y)$$ be $${y \over x} + \sec \left( {{y \over x}} \right),x > 0.$$
Then the equation of the curve is
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let y = y(x) be the solution of the differential equation

cosx$${{dy} \over {dx}}$$ + 2ysinx = sin2x, x $$ \in $$ $$\left( {0,{\pi \over 2}} \right)$$.

If y$$\left( {{\pi \over 3}} \right)$$ = 0, then y$$\left( {{\pi \over 4}} \right)$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
If x3dy + xy dx = x2dy + 2y dx; y(2) = e and
x > 1, then y(4) is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let $y=y(x)$ be the solution of the differential equation

$\left(3 y^{2}-5 x^{2}\right) y \mathrm{~d} x+2 x\left(x^{2}-y^{2}\right) \mathrm{d} y=0$

such that $y(1)=1$. Then $\left|(y(2))^{3}-12 y(2)\right|$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$y=y(x)$$ be the solution curve of the differential equation $$\sec y \frac{\mathrm{d} y}{\mathrm{~d} x}+2 x \sin y=x^3 \cos y, y(1)=0$$. Then $$y(\sqrt{3})$$ is equal to:
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$\alpha|x|=|y| \mathrm{e}^{x y-\beta}, \alpha, \beta \in \mathbf{N}$$ be the solution of the differential equation $$x \mathrm{~d} y-y \mathrm{~d} x+x y(x \mathrm{~d} y+y \mathrm{~d} x)=0,y(1)=2$$. Then $$\alpha+\beta$$ is equal to ________
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let $f: \mathbf{R} \rightarrow \mathbf{R}$ be such that $f(x y)=f(x) f(y)$, for all $x, y \in \mathbf{R}$ and $f(0) \neq 0$. Let $g:[1, \infty) \rightarrow \mathbf{R}$ be a differentiable function such that

$$ x^2 g(x)=\int_1^x\left(\mathrm{t}^2 f(\mathrm{t})-\operatorname{tg}(\mathrm{t})\right) d t $$

Then $g(2)$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$\int_\limits0^x \sqrt{1-\left(y^{\prime}(t)\right)^2} d t=\int_0^x y(t) d t, 0 \leq x \leq 3, y \geq 0, y(0)=0$$. Then at $$x=2, y^{\prime \prime}+y+1$$ is equal to
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: For a differentiable function $$f: \mathbb{R} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}$$, suppose $$f^{\prime}(x)=3 f(x)+\alpha$$, where $$\alpha \in \mathbb{R}, f(0)=1$$ and $$\lim _\limits{x \rightarrow-\infty} f(x)=7$$. Then $$9 f\left(-\log _e 3\right)$$ is equal to _________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let $y(x)$ be the solution of the differential equation

$$ x^2 \frac{d y}{d x}+x y=x^2+y^2, \quad x>\frac{1}{e} $$

satisfying $y(1)=0$. Then the value of $2 \frac{(y(e))^2}{y\left(e^2\right)}$ is ____________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
If y = y(x) is the solution of the differential equation
$${{dy} \over {dx}} = \left( {\tan x - y} \right){\sec ^2}x$$, $$x \in \left( { - {\pi \over 2},{\pi \over 2}} \right)$$,
such that y (0) = 0, then $$y\left( { - {\pi \over 4}} \right)$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let the solution curve $$y=f(x)$$ of the differential equation $$ \frac{d y}{d x}+\frac{x y}{x^{2}-1}=\frac{x^{4}+2 x}{\sqrt{1-x^{2}}}$$, $$x\in(-1,1)$$ pass through the origin. Then $$\int\limits_{-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}}^{\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}} f(x) d x $$ is equal to
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Suppose $$y=y(x)$$ be the solution curve to the differential equation $$\frac{d y}{d x}-y=2-e^{-x}$$ such that $$\lim\limits_{x \rightarrow \infty} y(x)$$ is finite. If $$a$$ and $$b$$ are respectively the $$x$$ - and $$y$$-intercepts of the tangent to the curve at $$x=0$$, then the value of $$a-4 b$$ is equal to _____________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The general solution of the differential equation $$\left(x-y^{2}\right) \mathrm{d} x+y\left(5 x+y^{2}\right) \mathrm{d} y=0$$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The slope of the tangent to a curve $$C: y=y(x)$$ at any point $$(x, y)$$ on it is $$\frac{2 \mathrm{e}^{2 x}-6 \mathrm{e}^{-x}+9}{2+9 \mathrm{e}^{-2 x}}$$. If $$C$$ passes through the points $$\left(0, \frac{1}{2}+\frac{\pi}{2 \sqrt{2}}\right)$$ and $$\left(\alpha, \frac{1}{2} \mathrm{e}^{2 \alpha}\right)$$, then $$\mathrm{e}^{\alpha}$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics
